content_markdown: An astronaut in orbit around Earth is said to be "weightless". This is due to the fact that the

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: gravitational force on the astronaut is zero.

options.label: B
options.text: astronaut and the spacecraft experience the same acceleration.

options.label: C
options.text: astronaut and the spacecraft experience the same gravitational force.

options.label: D
options.text: gravitational field at the position of the spacecraft is zero.

vibe_explanation: Weightlessness in orbit is a result of free-fall. Both the astronaut and the spacecraft are accelerating toward the center of the Earth at the same rate (the local gravitational acceleration $g$). Because they have the same acceleration, there is no normal contact force between the astronaut and the spacecraft walls/floor, which creates the sensation of weightlessness.
